headline: Nvidia, Microsoft, and Arm are all teasing Nvidia’s new N1X laptop processors
summary: Nvidia is set to announce Arm-powered laptop chips at Computex. Microsoft, Nvidia, and Arm are openly teasing a "new era of PC." The announcement is considered the world's worst-kept secret.

headline: SpaceX gets $4 billion contract to build missile-tracking ‘Golden Dome’ satellites
summary: SpaceX secured a $4.16 billion contract from the Pentagon. They will build missile-tracking satellites for the 'Golden Dome' defense system. These satellites will detect and track targets from space.

headline: Acer’s launching a Linux handheld for streaming your PC games
summary: Acer is releasing the Nitro Blaze Link, a Linux-based handheld device. It's designed as a 'streaming-first' companion for PC games. The device launches in Q4 2026 with a 7-inch display.

headline: Tech companies desperately want to film you doing chores
summary: An AI startup, Shift, offers free home cleaning in exchange for filming. The company plans to expand its services to other cities. The footage collected will be used for training AI systems.

headline: Marcia Lucas, Oscar-Winning Star Wars and Return of the Jedi Editor, Dies at 80
summary: Marcia Lucas, acclaimed film editor for Star Wars, has passed away at 80. She won an Academy Award for Best Film Editing for her work on Star Wars. Lucas was also the ex-wife of Star Wars creator George Lucas.

headline: No Spacesuits? No Problem! The For All Mankind Spin-Off Star City is a Very Different Kind of Space Exploration Show
summary: Star City, a spin-off of 'For All Mankind,' is exploring a new take on space exploration. Lead actor Rhys Ifans and showrunners discuss its unique premise. The series aims to tell the alt-history story from the Russian perspective.

headline: Toy Story 5 Has "One of the Most Heartbreaking Scenes" in the Franchise, Tom Hanks Says
summary: Tom Hanks hints at a deeply emotional scene in the upcoming Toy Story 5. He describes it as "one of the most heartbreaking scenes" in the franchise's history. The film continues the 30-year legacy of the beloved Pixar series.

headline: Spider-Man: Brand New Day's Punisher Is "Authentic to the Frank Castle We Know," Tom Holland Says
summary: Tom Holland assures fans that "Spider-Man: Brand New Day" will feature an authentic Punisher. He addressed concerns about the character being softened in the new comic series. Holland emphasizes that Frank Castle will remain true to his established persona.

headline: Palace handed Andrew's controversial envoy emails six years ago
summary: Thousands of emails about Prince Andrew's financial dealings were given to the Royal Household. The transfer of information occurred in 2020. These emails concern the former prince's controversial activities.

headline: No deal announced after Trump meeting to make 'final determination' on Iran
summary: President Trump met advisers to decide on Iran, but no deal was announced. Officials had confirmed a framework agreement between the US and Iran. The meeting was for a "final determination" regarding the situation.

headline: Arrive three hours before flight home, airline boss tells UK holidaymakers
summary: Wizz Air CEO advises UK holidaymakers to arrive three hours early for flights. This recommendation follows reports of passengers missing flights due to long queues. The advice aims to prevent missed return or connecting flights.

headline: Attorney general had 'no doubt' on reviewing teen boys' rape sentences
summary: Lord Hermer, the Attorney General, had no hesitation reviewing teen boys' rape sentences. He sought details quickly after learning rapists were spared custody. The review addresses concerns over the leniency of the initial sentences.
